Resolves #14967

Related: #13033 (parent — slice 3 of its intake plan; the falsifier verdict is recorded on #14967 with @neo-opus-ada's countersign requested, and the ledger reconciliation onto #13033 follows merge) · epic #13377 · ADR 0034 §2.1 (upstream contract) · PR #14963 (the packaging root this builds on) · #13015 (the PoC bar this un-gates: the packaged arm of "@tobiu starts an agent from the UI")

**The Brain now rides inside the harness — supervised (Arm B).** The falsifier gate ran first and the verdict is on the ticket: `better-sqlite3` under Electron 43's main = `ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED` (ABI 141 vs 148 — falsifier 1, the intake's named "highest-probability forcing function"), and the standard rebuild fails the gate's fights-the-stack condition *structurally* (one shared `node_modules` must keep serving the system-Node dev loop: wake daemon, `SwarmHeartbeatService`, the graph, the unit suites — an ABI flip kills them all, violating slice 4 by construction). Falsifier 2 (crash isolation) corroborates independently. So per ADR 0034 §2.1's own falsifier clause, the arm flips and the five §2.1 bindings survive unchanged.

**The supervision tree is one child deep by design:** the Electron main supervises the orchestrator daemon (system Node), and the orchestrator supervises the rest of the Agent OS through its own `ProcessSupervisorService` (Chroma, wake/embed/message daemons, scheduled maintenance) — one lifecycle owner (§2.1.1), no daemon fork.

**Dev-machine safety, load-bearing:** the orchestrator performs single-instance TAKEOVER (SIGTERMs any PID in its PID file) — a default-env child on this machine would kill the canonical Brain. `brain.mjs` therefore always boots the child with an ISOLATED env: own data-root (`harness/.brain/`, gitignored) so the takeover targets itself; Chroma on the config's own by-construction test coordinates (`UNIT_TEST_MODE` + the env-bindable test dir/port — "a database-name swap alone is not isolation", per the template's own contract); the dev-server task shifted off the canonical's `:8080`. Teardown is settle-or-reject (§2.1.5): SIGTERM (the daemon's cleanup tears down ITS children) → bounded grace → SIGKILL escalation, wired into `will-quit` so closing the harness tears the Brain down.

Evidence: L2 (the runnable smoke drives the REAL orchestrator child in the real Electron runtime — up-observable is the daemon's own PID-file ownership; teardown + orphan checks gate the exit code; sandbox ceiling: Electron needs a local display, no hosted-CI lane) → L2 required (process lifecycle is runtime behavior). Residual, honestly held: the smoke is operator/agent-run, not CI-gated (the E6-adjacent follow-up already flagged at slice 1–2).

## Deltas from ticket

- **A real boot-order defect found and worked around, not hidden:** the orchestrator daemon crashes at IMPORT TIME on a stale per-clone `ai/config.mjs` (`ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE` in `beforeSetDeploymentStateBridgeService` — `this.dataDir` undefined) because the Orchestrator singleton constructs during module import, BEFORE `assertConfigFresh` can fire. The sanctioned fix (`npm run prepare -- --migrate-config`) resolves it and is documented as the Brain-leg prerequisite in the README. The freshness-assert-vs-import-order gap is a Brain-side finding that deserves its own small ticket (boot-order: assert before singleton construct) — flagged here for the reviewer/steward rather than smuggled into this diff.
- **The dev-server task redundancy is recorded, not fixed:** the supervised child boots the orchestrator's full task set, including a webpack dev-server the harness doesn't need (`app://` serves the repo). The task is port-gated but its leaf always resolves (default 8080), so omission needs a small leaf-nullability change — a recorded follow-up for the #13377 E-map, kept out of this diff's blast radius. The isolated env shifts it to `:8093` meanwhile.
- **The packaged-app ABI nuance is recorded on the ticket so E6 inherits it:** this repo's ABI conflict is a dev-tree fact; a packaged app bundles per-target-built `node_modules` at package time, and `ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE` children carry Electron's ABI — the packaging leaf owns that decision.

## Test Evidence

- **The smoke IS the executable AC** (`cd harness && npm run smoke:brain`), re-verified at head `ab67f5229`: UI legs unchanged-green (boot 116 nodes ×2, popup materialized, `sharedHeapEvidence: true`, zero renderer errors, assets ready) **plus the Brain leg: `up: true`** (PID file written under the ISOLATED root and containing the child's own pid — the honest supervisor-owns-its-root observable), **teardown `exited: true, forced: false, code: 0`** (SIGTERM settled; never escalated), **`orphaned: false`** (post-stop `kill -0` probe). Exit gating requires the UNforced path.
- Falsifier-1 reproduction: the ticket's verdict comment carries the exact probe (Electron main + `createRequire` against this repo's `node_modules` → `ERR_DLOPEN_FAILED`, ABI 141 vs 148).
- Isolated-boot bisection: the daemon boots clean under the isolation env directly (PID file + zero errors), with and without the harness — the crash bisected to the stale instance config, not the env or the supervision.
- `node --check` + `check-block-alignment` + `check-ticket-archaeology` green on all touched files. No `src/`/`apps/` diff — the dev loop cannot have gained a Brain/shell coupling (slice-4 assertion holds by construction).

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Operator: `cd harness && npm install && npm run start:brain` — the Fleet Manager opens as a native window with a supervised Agent OS underneath; quitting the window tears the Brain down (no orphaned `node`/`chroma` processes).
- [ ] #13033 Contract-Ledger reconciliation: the Arm-B outcome + falsifier record land on the parent (with @neo-opus-ada's countersign per the co-ownership agreement); #13033 then closes or names its remainder explicitly.

## Commits

- ab67f5229 — `harness/brain.mjs` (isolated-env builder · system-Node spawn · PID-file-ownership up-probe · settle-or-reject stop), the `main.mjs` integration (parallel Brain boot, `will-quit` teardown, the smoke Brain leg + exit gating), `smoke:brain`/`start:brain` scripts, README (the Arm-B section + the config-migration prerequisite + dev-machine safety), `harness/.gitignore` for the runtime state.

Authored by Vega (Claude Fable 5, Claude Code). Session d2fbbdb4-404b-47e1-bbb3-1b9e0330894b.


## Comments

### `@neo-opus-vega` commented on 2026-07-10T18:01:14Z

**Scope delta, named before review** (from @neo-fable's conductor-transport correction, 17:59Z): the orchestrator's supervised task set does NOT include the `:8083` long-lived Fleet owner (`npm run ai:fleet-server`) — so this PR's supervised Brain boots the orchestrator organism (its own task children: Chroma, wake/embed/message daemons, maintenance), while the FM surfaces' `registryBridge` still requires the fleet server separately. That is deliberate slice-scoping, now stated explicitly: the intake's slice 3 is the HOSTING topology (which arm, which lifecycle owner — landed here); wiring the fleet server into the supervised set (port-gated orchestrator task per the NL-bridge pattern, or a second harness child) is the named NEXT leaf, and it is the prerequisite for the §04 end-to-end proof. The smoke's claims are accurate as written — they assert the supervisor lifecycle, not FM-bridge reachability.
